seo.title: 25 Best Things To Do in Chiang Mai (2026 Guide) — Temples, Treks & Markets
seo.metaDescription: Discover the 25 best things to do in Chiang Mai in 2026. From Doi Suthep temple to ethical elephant sanctuaries, cooking classes, and night markets.

intro: Chiang Mai packs more unforgettable experiences per square kilometer than almost anywhere in Southeast Asia. Between the ancient temples of the moat-encircled Old City, the misty peaks of Doi Inthanon, and the aromatic chaos of the Sunday Walking Street Market, you could spend weeks here and still discover something new. Here are 25 things worth your time — from must-sees to local secrets.

activities.name: Climb the 306 Steps to Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
activities.description: Chiang Mai's most iconic temple sits atop Doi Suthep mountain. Climb the naga-flanked staircase (or take the funicular) to reach the golden chedi, then soak in panoramic views of the city below. Time your visit for late afternoon to catch the monks' evening chanting.
tips: Take a songthaew from the zoo gate — 60 THB/person shared, or 300 THB chartered
Wear long pants and cover shoulders — wraps available to borrow at the entrance
activities.bestTime: Late afternoon for sunset views and evening chanting

activities.name: Explore the Old City Temples on Foot
activities.description: Chiang Mai's compact Old City within the moat holds dozens of temples you can explore in a single morning. Hit the big three — Wat Chedi Luang (ruined 14th-century chedi), Wat Phra Singh (classic Lanna architecture), and Wat Chiang Man (oldest temple in the city, founded 1296).
tips: Start early (7–8 AM) before the heat kicks in
Join the free monk chat at Wat Chedi Luang in the afternoon

activities.name: Take a Thai Cooking Class
activities.description: Chiang Mai is Thailand's cooking class capital. Most half-day classes start with a guided market tour to buy fresh ingredients, then teach 4–5 dishes including pad thai, green curry, and tom yum. You eat everything you cook.
tips: Mama Noi's, Thai Farm, and Asia Scenic are consistently top-rated
Morning classes are less crowded than afternoon sessions

activities.name: Visit an Ethical Elephant Sanctuary
activities.description: Several sanctuaries around Chiang Mai offer the chance to feed, bathe, and walk alongside rescued elephants in a natural setting — no riding, chains, or hooks. Elephant Jungle Sanctuary and Elephant Nature Park are the most reputable options.
activities.price: 1,800–3,500 THB (~$50–100 USD) including transport and lunch
tips: Book at least 2–3 days ahead in peak season (Nov–Feb)
Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy — the mud bath is the highlight

activities.name: Browse the Sunday Walking Street Market
activities.description: Every Sunday from 4 PM to midnight, Ratchadamnoen Road becomes Thailand's largest pedestrian market. Hundreds of stalls sell handmade Lanna crafts, hill tribe textiles, local art, and some of the best street food in the north. The side sois (alleys) hide the best food vendors.
tips: The stretch near Wat Phra Singh has the best craft vendors
Budget 200–500 THB for street food — try the grilled sai ua sausage
activities.bestTime: Arrive by 5 PM before peak crowds

activities.name: Trek to Doi Inthanon Summit and Waterfalls
activities.description: Thailand's highest peak offers cloud forests, twin royal chedis with manicured gardens, the impressive Wachirathan waterfall, and hill tribe villages. The Kew Mae Pan nature trail at the summit is a 3 km loop through mossy cloud forest.
activities.price: 300 THB (~$8.50 USD) park entrance, guide 1,000–2,000 THB
tips: Bring a jacket — summit temperatures can drop to 5°C even in hot season
The Kew Mae Pan trail requires a local guide (mandatory, included in some tours)
activities.bestTime: November to February for clearest skies

activities.name: Eat Your Way Through Khao Soi Stalls
activities.description: Chiang Mai's signature dish is khao soi — egg noodles in a rich coconut curry broth, topped with crispy fried noodles and served with pickled mustard greens and shallots. The best bowls cost under 60 THB at local restaurants.
tips: Khao Soi Khun Yai (Grandma's Khao Soi) near the north gate is legendary
Khao Soi Mae Sai on Ratchaphakinai Road is another local favorite
activities.bestTime: Lunch (11 AM – 2 PM, many close when sold out)

activities.name: Explore the Chiang Mai Night Bazaar
activities.description: This nightly market along Chang Khlan Road is Chiang Mai's original tourist market, running every evening from 6 PM. Browse handicrafts, clothing, and souvenirs across the main building, Anusarn Market (best for food), and Kalare Night Bazaar.
tips: Haggling is expected — start at 50% of the asking price
Anusarn Market has the best food court with Thai, Indian, and international options
activities.bestTime: After 7 PM when all stalls are open

activities.name: Hike to Wat Pha Lat (Hidden Jungle Temple)
activities.description: This serene forest temple halfway up Doi Suthep is reached via the Monk's Trail — a shaded jungle path starting near Chiang Mai University. Far less visited than Doi Suthep temple, it features moss-covered chedis, flowing streams, and birdsong.
tips: Start from the Monk's Trail trailhead near the zoo
Bring water and mosquito repellent — it's humid under the canopy

activities.name: Visit the Chiang Mai Night Safari
activities.description: An open-air nocturnal wildlife park with tram rides through Savanna Safari and Predator Prowl zones. Over 130 species including giraffes, zebras, and white tigers. The walking Jaguar Trail zone is open during daytime too.
tips: The 7 PM tram is less packed than the 8:30 PM departure
Combined day + night tickets offer better value

activities.name: Cafe-Hop Through Nimmanhaemin Road
activities.description: Nimman is Chiang Mai's trendiest neighborhood with dozens of specialty coffee shops, design boutiques, and art galleries. Sois 1–17 each have their own character. Ristr8to won the World Latte Art Championship — try their signature latte.
tips: Ristr8to (Soi 3), Graph Cafe, and Ponganes are local favorites
Visit on a weekday to grab seats at popular spots

activities.name: Join a Muay Thai Training Session
activities.description: Several gyms in Chiang Mai offer drop-in Muay Thai classes for beginners. Lanna Muay Thai and Team Quest are well-known for welcoming first-timers. Sessions last 1.5–2 hours and include pad work, bag drills, and clinch techniques.
tips: Bring your own hand wraps or buy them at the gym (100 THB)
Morning sessions (8–10 AM) are less intense than the afternoon grind

activities.name: Explore Bua Tong Sticky Waterfalls
activities.description: One of Chiang Mai's most unique natural attractions — limestone deposits make the rock surface sticky enough to walk up the cascading waterfalls barefoot. Located in Sri Lanna National Park, about 1.5 hours north of the city.
activities.duration: Half day (including 1.5 hr drive each way)
tips: Wear water shoes or go barefoot — the limestone is surprisingly grippy
Combine with a visit to Mae Ngat Dam for a floating raft experience
activities.bestTime: Rainy season (Jul–Oct) for strongest water flow

activities.name: Watch Sunset at Wat Umong
activities.description: A 14th-century forest temple with unique underground tunnels (umong means tunnel). The grounds include a fasting Buddha statue, a lake, and a large stupa set among tall trees. Far more peaceful than the Old City temples.
tips: Walk through the tunnels first, then explore the forested grounds
The area around the lake is perfect for quiet meditation

activities.name: Try Sai Ua (Northern Thai Sausage)
activities.description: Chiang Mai's signature sausage is packed with lemongrass, galangal, kaffir lime, and chili. Find it grilled to perfection at market stalls throughout the city. Best eaten with sticky rice and fresh vegetables.
tips: SP Chicken near the north gate is famous for both sai ua and grilled chicken
The Saturday and Sunday markets have the freshest grilled sausage

activities.name: Go White Water Rafting on the Mae Taeng River
activities.description: The Mae Taeng river north of Chiang Mai offers class 3–4 rapids through jungle scenery. Full-day trips typically combine rafting with bamboo raft drifting and often include a jungle trek or zip-line session.
activities.price: 1,500–2,500 THB (~$42–70 USD) including transport and lunch
tips: July to November has the strongest rapids
Pack a dry bag for your phone and camera

activities.name: Visit the Royal Park Rajapruek
activities.description: A 200-acre botanical garden featuring the Royal Pavilion (Ho Kham Luang), themed international gardens, and seasonal flower displays. Particularly spectacular during the December–February flower festival with millions of blooms.
tips: Rent an electric tram (50 THB) to cover the vast grounds efficiently
Visit during the Flower Festival (Dec–Feb) for the best displays

activities.name: Sample Street Food at Chiang Mai Gate Market
activities.description: The South Gate market is a local favorite open daily from early morning to midnight. Morning stalls serve khao kha moo (braised pork leg on rice) and jok (rice porridge), while evening vendors grill sai ua, satay, and fresh seafood.
tips: Morning is best for authentic Thai breakfast dishes
The lady at the far-right corner of the main area makes the best khao kha moo

activities.name: Zip-Line Through the Jungle Canopy
activities.description: Several operators offer zip-line courses through the rainforest canopy surrounding Chiang Mai. Flight of the Gibbon and Eagle Track are the most established, with courses including sky bridges, abseiling, and platforms 50+ meters high.
tips: Flight of the Gibbon has the longest course (over 5 km of cables)
Book directly for the best price — avoid hotel markup

activities.name: Visit the Chiang Mai City Arts & Cultural Center
activities.description: Housed in a beautiful 1924 colonial building on the main Old City square, this museum tells the story of Chiang Mai from its founding in 1296 through the Lanna Kingdom to the present day. Well-curated exhibits with English descriptions.
tips: The combined ticket (180 THB) includes the Lanna Folklife Museum next door
Air-conditioned — a good midday escape from the heat

activities.name: Learn Traditional Thai Massage
activities.description: Chiang Mai is one of the best places in Thailand to study traditional Thai massage. The Old Medicine Hospital offers 5-day courses (30 hours) that teach the fundamentals of northern-style Thai massage, including certification.
tips: The Old Medicine Hospital near the Old City south gate is the most established school
One-day intro courses are available if you just want a taster

activities.name: Drive the Samoeng Loop
activities.description: A scenic 100 km motorcycle loop through mountain roads northwest of Chiang Mai, passing rice paddies, hill tribe villages, orchid farms, and the Queen Sirikit Botanic Garden. One of the best day rides in northern Thailand.
tips: Drive counter-clockwise (via Route 1096) for the best sequence of views
Stop at the Mon Cham viewpoint restaurant for hilltop Hmong strawberry farms

activities.name: Attend the Yi Peng Lantern Festival
activities.description: Each November (around the full moon), thousands of paper lanterns are released into the night sky over Chiang Mai during the Yi Peng festival. The main official event at Mae Jo University is ticketed, but lanterns float throughout the city.
activities.price: Free in the city, 3,000–5,000 THB for organized events at Mae Jo
tips: Book Mae Jo tickets months in advance — they sell out
The Loy Krathong water lantern festival happens on the same dates along the Ping River

activities.name: Explore the Warorot Market (Kad Luang)
activities.description: Chiang Mai's biggest and oldest local market near the Ping River. Three floors of northern Thai food products, dried fruits, spices, Lanna textiles, and everyday goods. This is where locals actually shop — an authentic counterpoint to the tourist night markets.
tips: The basement food floor has the best prices for dried mango and northern Thai snacks
The flower market across the street is worth a quick look

activities.name: Get a Traditional Thai Massage
activities.description: Chiang Mai has some of the best and cheapest Thai massages in the country. A 1-hour traditional Thai massage costs 200–300 THB at local shops. Lila Thai Massage employs and trains former female prisoners — great massages with a social mission.
tips: Lila Thai Massage has multiple branches — the Old City location is easiest to find
Avoid places that offer 'special' massages — stick to reputable shops

travelTips: Download the Grab app before arriving — it's the most reliable way to get fair-priced transport
Carry a light rain jacket year-round — even dry season gets occasional evening showers in the mountains
Most temples are free but the handful that charge (Doi Suthep, Chedi Luang) are worth the 40–100 THB fee
Learn to say 'khop khun khrap/ka' (thank you) — northern Thais especially appreciate polite foreigners
ATMs charge 220 THB per withdrawal — take out larger amounts to minimize fees
The Saturday and Sunday Walking Street markets are in different locations — Saturday is on Wua Lai Road, Sunday on Ratchadamnoen Road
Book ethical elephant sanctuaries directly through their websites to avoid middleman markup
If visiting March–April, check air quality index (AQI) daily — consider wearing an N95 mask outdoors
